{{Quote|Scuttle-butt is he took out the anti-orbital ion cannons of Spefik single-handed.|An [[TIE pilot|Imperial pilot]], on [[Ranulf Trommer/Legends|Ranulf Trommer]]|<ref name="RoC" />}}
'''Spefik''' was a barren, rocky [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Atrivis sector/Legends|Atrivis sector]] of the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. The planet was home to a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] [[Spefik base|base]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]; however, the base was destroyed by [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] forces in the [[battle of Spefik]] around [[0 ABY/Legends|0.5 ABY]]. [[TIE pilot|Imperial pilot]] [[Ranulf Trommer/Legends|Ranulf Trommer]] distinguished himself in that battle.

==Description==
Spefik was a desolate, barren, rocky [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]],<ref name="SWR" /> located in the [[Spefik system/Legends|Spefik system]] of the [[Atrivis sector/Legends|Atrivis sector]].<ref name="Atlas" />

==History==
During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Spefik was home to a [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] group fighting against the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]]. They protected their [[Spefik base|base]] with anti-orbital [[Ion cannon/Legends|ion cannons]]; however, this defense ultimately proved insufficient against the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]]. During the [[battle of Spefik]] in [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], sometime shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|destruction]] of the first [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]] over [[Yavin 4/Legends|Yavin 4]], Imperial forces attacked the planet with at least two [[Fighter wing/Legends|fighter wings]], destroying the cannons. Elite [[TIE pilot|Imperial pilot]] [[Ranulf Trommer/Legends|Ranulf Trommer]] played a prominent role in the battle and was later colloquially credited with single-handedly destroying the cannons, although he demurred from claiming the credit. After that victory, Trommer was transferred to the [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] ''[[Ravagor]]'' and fought in the [[Battle of Aguarl 3]].<ref name="RoC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Spefik was first mentioned in the [[Dark Horse Comics|Dark Horse]] comic [[River of Chaos 1|''River of Chaos'' 1]] ([[1995]]), written by [[Louise Simonson]]. It provided part of the backstory of Imperial pilot Ranulf Trommer. His exploits at the battle helped establish his elite skills. The planet was later included in the [[1998]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars: Rebellion (video game)|''Star Wars: Rebellion'']], which placed it within the Atrivis sector, although the game's encyclopedia entry for Spefik mistakenly spelled the [[Sector/Legends|sector]] "Artivas." ''Star Wars: Rebellion'' also provided an image of the planet; however, this image was also shared by 16 other worlds. Spefik was mentioned in the ''[[Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' (1998), and, later, ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ([[2008]]) repeated this entry verbatim.

===''Star Wars: Rebellion'' cartography===
''Star Wars: Rebellion'' provided travel times between planets within the same sector; however, on a number of occasions, these were significantly longer than other [[canon]]ical sources. As such, these travel times seem to reflect game mechanics rather than canonical travel times. Nonetheless, the times given for Spefik were as follows:

!width="60"|Despayre

|}
